title: Hannah's prayer and Samuel's birth
summary_text: Hannah pours out her grief before the LORD at Shiloh and vows that if God gives her a son she will dedicate him to lifelong service. Eli misreads her silent prayer at first, then blesses her, and the LORD opens her womb. Samuel is born, weaned, and brought to the sanctuary as a living answer to prayer.
key_points: Hannah prays from deep distress.
She vows to give her son to the LORD.
Eli blesses her after misunderstanding her prayer.
Samuel is born as an answer to faith.
He is brought to serve at Shiloh.
evidence_phrases: remember me
I have poured out my soul before the LORD
I will give him to the LORD
the LORD remembered her
as long as he lives

title: Hannah's song and Eli's warning
summary_text: Hannah praises the LORD for overturning the proud and lifting the humble, setting the tone for the whole book. The chapter then contrasts her faith with the corruption of Eli's sons, who abuse sacrifices and despise the LORD's offering. Samuel grows before God while a man of God warns Eli that his house will be judged for dishonoring the sanctuary.
key_points: Hannah celebrates the LORD's holy reversals.
Eli's sons exploit the sacrifices.
Samuel grows in favor with God and people.
A prophetic word condemns Eli's house.
The chapter contrasts faithful worship with abuse.
evidence_phrases: my horn is exalted in the LORD
the LORD kills and makes alive
the sons of Eli were sons of Belial
the child Samuel grew before the LORD
those who despise me shall be lightly esteemed

title: Samuel called by name
summary_text: Samuel hears the LORD calling in the night, and after Eli's help he learns to answer, "Speak, for your servant hears." The message confirms judgment on Eli's house and cannot be hidden. Samuel grows into a trustworthy prophet whose words are established throughout Israel.
key_points: The LORD calls Samuel in the night.
Eli helps Samuel recognize the voice.
God confirms judgment on Eli's house.
Samuel is established as a prophet.
Nothing the LORD says falls to the ground.
evidence_phrases: Samuel, Samuel
Speak, for your servant hears
I will do a thing in Israel
the LORD revealed himself to Samuel
the LORD was with him

title: The ark captured and Eli dies
summary_text: Israel treats the ark like a talisman and marches it into battle, but the Philistines rout the army and capture the ark. Eli falls backward and dies when he hears the news, and his daughter-in-law names her son Ichabod because the glory has departed from Israel. The chapter is a stark warning that sacred objects cannot replace covenant faithfulness.
key_points: Israel brings the ark into battle without repentance.
The Philistines defeat Israel and seize the ark.
Eli dies upon hearing the news.
Ichabod marks the departure of glory.
The chapter shows religious presumption ending in loss.
evidence_phrases: the ark of the covenant of the LORD
let us fetch the ark
the glory is departed from Israel
she named the child Ichabod
his heart trembled for the ark of God

title: The ark among the Philistines
summary_text: The Philistines place the ark in Dagon's temple, but Dagon falls before it and the people are struck with affliction. Town after town suffers because the ark brings the LORD's presence into enemy territory. The chapter makes clear that the captured ark is not captive at all; it is still ruling.
key_points: Dagon falls before the ark.
The Philistines are afflicted wherever the ark goes.
Their cities cry out under judgment.
The ark exposes the weakness of idols.
The enemy learns that Israel's God is not defeated.
evidence_phrases: Dagon fell on his face
the hand of the LORD was heavy
the ark of the God of Israel shall not remain with us
tumors sprang up
the cry of the city went up to heaven

title: The ark returned
summary_text: The Philistines send the ark back on a new cart with guilt offerings, testing whether the plague came from God or chance. The cows head straight for Beth-shemesh, confirming that the LORD is guiding the return. Israel rejoices but also learns reverence when some men look into the ark and are struck down.
key_points: The ark returns with a guilt offering.
The cows head directly to Israel's town.
God proves the plague was his hand.
Rejoicing is tempered by reverence.
Holy things must not be treated casually.
evidence_phrases: make a new cart
the LORD has done us this great evil
the cows took the straight way
the hand of God was very heavy there
who is able to stand before this holy LORD God

title: Samuel at Mizpah
summary_text: Samuel calls Israel to put away foreign gods and gather at Mizpah in repentance and prayer. As the Philistines come up against them, Samuel cries to the LORD, and God thunders on the enemy while Israel pursues the victory. The chapter ends with Samuel judging Israel and setting up memorials to the LORD's help.
key_points: Israel turns from idols at Samuel's word.
They gather for prayer and confession.
The LORD fights with thunder from heaven.
The Philistines are subdued for a time.
Samuel is established as judge and prophet.
evidence_phrases: put away the foreign gods
pour out water before the LORD
the LORD thundered with a great thunder
Ebenezer
Samuel judged Israel all the days of his life

title: Israel demands a king
summary_text: When Samuel grows old and his sons do not walk in his ways, Israel asks for a king like the nations around them. Samuel is grieved, but the LORD tells him that the request is really a rejection of God's own rule. Samuel warns them about royal cost and burden, yet the people persist and the monarchy becomes the answer to their demand.
key_points: Samuel's sons fail as judges.
Israel wants a king to rule them.
The request is treated as rejection of God.
Samuel warns about the cost of monarchy.
The people still insist on a king.
evidence_phrases: make us a king to judge us
they have not rejected you, but they have rejected me
the manner of the king
he will take
we will have a king over us

title: Saul seeks the donkeys
summary_text: Saul searches for his father's lost donkeys and is led by providence to Samuel instead. The LORD tells Samuel to anoint the man from Benjamin, and Samuel identifies Saul as the chosen one with honor at the feast. The chapter quietly introduces Saul as a tall and promising king-to-be, even before he knows it himself.
key_points: A donkey search leads Saul to Samuel.
God tells Samuel the chosen man is coming.
Saul is honored at the feast.
Samuel speaks of the kingdom in private terms.
The future king is still unaware of his calling.
evidence_phrases: the LORD had told Samuel
the man whom I spoke to you of
he shall reign over my people
the choicest portion
do you not know that the best is reserved

title: Saul anointed and confirmed
summary_text: Samuel anoints Saul, gives him signs, and tells him that the Spirit will come upon him as proof of God's choice. Saul prophesies with the prophets, and the people marvel because the change is so unmistakable. Yet Saul also hides, hinting that his kingship will be marked by uncertainty as well as promise.
key_points: Samuel privately anoints Saul.
Several signs confirm the message.
Saul is filled with prophetic power.
He is publicly identified by lot.
Some still despise his rule.
evidence_phrases: the Spirit of the LORD will come mightily on you
another man
God gave him another heart
Is Saul also among the prophets
there was no one like him among all the people

title: Saul rescues Jabesh-gilead
summary_text: When Nahash the Ammonite threatens Jabesh-gilead, Saul is moved by the Spirit and gathers Israel to rescue the city. The victory unites the tribes and confirms Saul's public kingship at Gilgal. The chapter shows the best side of Saul: courage, mercy, and the ability to rally a divided people.
key_points: Saul responds decisively to the threat.
Israel comes together for battle.
The city is rescued from humiliation.
Saul's kingship is renewed at Gilgal.
The people rejoice in deliverance.
evidence_phrases: the Spirit of God came mightily on Saul
tomorrow by this time
there is no one to save us
the LORD has worked salvation in Israel
they made Saul king before the LORD

title: Samuel's farewell speech
summary_text: Samuel reviews his leadership, insists that he has taken no bribe, and reminds Israel how the LORD repeatedly rescued them from enemies. He warns that asking for a king does not remove accountability, and he calls both ruler and people to fear and serve the LORD. The speech closes the era of judges with a moral and spiritual summary.
key_points: Samuel defends his integrity.
He recounts the LORD's saving acts.
The people acknowledge their sin in asking for a king.
Thunder and rain confirm Samuel's word.
The chapter urges fear and service to God.
evidence_phrases: whose ox have I taken
the LORD sent Jerubbaal
if you will fear the LORD
we have added to all our sins
only fear the LORD

title: Saul's unlawful sacrifice
summary_text: Saul grows anxious when the Philistines mass and Samuel does not arrive on time, so he offers the burnt offering himself. Samuel rebukes him for acting foolishly and announces that his kingdom will not endure. The chapter marks the first clear break between Saul's kingship and God's enduring purpose.
key_points: Saul panics under military pressure.
He offers sacrifice without waiting for Samuel.
Samuel declares the kingdom will not endure.
Israel's weapon shortage deepens the crisis.
Saul's fear leads to permanent loss.
evidence_phrases: you have not kept the commandment of the LORD
I forced myself therefore
your kingdom shall not continue
the people were distressed
there was no smith found

title: Jonathan's victory and rash oath
summary_text: Jonathan and his armor bearer trust the LORD and spark a panic among the Philistines, showing that faith can win where armies hesitate. Saul's rash oath, however, weakens the people and nearly costs Jonathan his life after the king's own son tastes honey. The chapter contrasts brave initiative with foolish religion and ends with Saul's damaged leadership.
key_points: Jonathan attacks by faith, not numbers.
The Philistines panic and turn on one another.
Saul binds the people with a foolish oath.
Jonathan is nearly punished for eating honey.
The chapter reveals both courage and confusion.
evidence_phrases: there is no restraint to the LORD to save
the LORD has delivered them into the hand of Israel
cursed be the man that eats food until evening
you have troubled the land
Jonathan had not heard his father swear

title: Saul rejected for Amalek
summary_text: Samuel commands Saul to destroy Amalek completely, but Saul spares the best livestock and keeps King Agag alive. When confronted, Saul blames the people, but Samuel declares that obedience is better than sacrifice and that the LORD has rejected Saul as king. The chapter ends with Agag's death and Samuel's grief over Saul's failure.
key_points: Saul disobeys the command to devote Amalek to destruction.
He excuses his failure with spiritual language.
Samuel announces God's rejection of Saul.
Agag is killed after Samuel's rebuke.
Obedience is declared better than sacrifice.
evidence_phrases: to obey is better than sacrifice
the LORD has rejected you
Saul and the people spared Agag
I have performed the commandment of the LORD
the LORD regretted that he had made Saul king

title: David is anointed
summary_text: The LORD sends Samuel to Bethlehem to anoint a new king from Jesse's sons, and David is chosen because the LORD looks on the heart. The Spirit comes upon David, while an evil spirit troubles Saul and leads to the introduction of David as a musician in the court. The chapter quietly begins the rise of Israel's future king.
key_points: Samuel is sent to Bethlehem.
David is chosen over his brothers.
The Spirit of the LORD comes on David.
Saul needs David's music for relief.
God is already preparing a replacement king.
evidence_phrases: man looks on the outward appearance
the LORD looks on the heart
the Spirit of the LORD came mightily on David
the Spirit of the LORD departed from Saul
David took a harp

title: David and Goliath
summary_text: David comes to the battlefield with bread for his brothers and ends up facing Goliath, the giant who mocks the armies of the living God. David declares that the battle belongs to the LORD, strikes Goliath with a sling stone, and wins a decisive victory with the giant's own sword. The chapter celebrates faith that sees God's honor where others see only impossible odds.
key_points: David hears Goliath's challenge.
He refuses Saul's armor and trusts the LORD.
The giant falls to a sling stone.
David finishes the fight with Goliath's own sword.
Israel wins because the LORD saves.
evidence_phrases: who is this uncircumcised Philistine
the battle is the LORD's
I come to you in the name of the LORD
with a sling and with a stone
there is no sword like it

title: David and Jonathan's covenant
summary_text: Jonathan loves David as his own soul, and the two men form a covenant that lasts beyond politics. David succeeds in everything he does, which makes Saul jealous and afraid as the people's praise shifts toward the young warrior. The chapter moves from friendship to rising royal tension.
key_points: Jonathan and David bind themselves in covenant.
David's victories deepen Saul's jealousy.
Michal becomes part of the royal story.
Saul fears David's popularity.
David keeps prospering under the LORD.
evidence_phrases: the soul of Jonathan was knit with the soul of David
Saul eyed David from that day
David behaved himself wisely
the women sang
Saul became David's enemy continually

title: Saul's attempts on David
summary_text: Saul orders David killed, but Jonathan intercedes and briefly restores peace between them. When Saul's rage returns, David escapes through a window and Michal helps him flee while pretending he is ill. The chapter shows how family ties and loyal friendship still cannot overcome Saul's growing hostility.
key_points: Jonathan successfully pleads for David.
Saul's hostility returns after a brief peace.
Michal helps David escape by night.
David flees to Samuel at Ramah.
God keeps David from Saul's hand.
evidence_phrases: Jonathan delighted much in David
Saul sought to pin David to the wall
Michal let David down through the window
the Spirit of God was on the messengers
Is Saul also among the prophets

title: David and Jonathan's covenant again
summary_text: David and Jonathan use a secret sign at the feast to determine Saul's intent, and Saul's murderous anger is confirmed. The two friends renew their covenant and part with grief, knowing the kingdom's conflict will separate them. The chapter deepens the tragedy by showing loyalty trapped inside royal violence.
key_points: Jonathan tests Saul's intentions by a sign.
Saul's anger proves David is in danger.
The friends renew their covenant.
David and Jonathan part in tears.
The kingdom's future now feels fractured.
evidence_phrases: the LORD is between me and you forever
why should he be slain
Saul's anger was kindled
go in peace
we have sworn both of us in the name of the LORD

title: Nob, bread, and Gath
summary_text: David arrives at Nob hungry and receives the holy bread and Goliath's sword from Ahimelech, but the encounter later costs the priests their lives. He then flees to Gath, where he pretends madness to survive among the Philistines. The chapter shows David in exile, scrambling for food, weapons, and safety while Saul's violence expands.
key_points: David receives bread and a sword at Nob.
Doeg's report leads to priestly slaughter.
David escapes to Gath.
He feigns madness to avoid death.
Saul's paranoia spreads beyond him.
evidence_phrases: the bread of the presence
the sword of Goliath
he changed his behavior
they drove him away
David escaped

title: Adullam and the priests killed
summary_text: David gathers a band of distressed followers in the cave of Adullam while Saul's suspicion turns into murderous paranoia. Doeg tells Saul that Ahimelech helped David, and the king orders the priests of Nob killed, deepening the bloodguilt on his house. Abiathar escapes to David, linking the outlaw king and the surviving priesthood.
key_points: David gathers the afflicted at Adullam.
Saul accuses the priests of conspiracy.
Doeg carries out the slaughter at Nob.
Abiathar escapes to David with the ephod.
Saul's violence becomes indiscriminate.

why have you conspired against me
Doeg the Edomite turned
I have occasioned the death of all the persons
remain with me

title: Keilah and the Ziphites
summary_text: David rescues Keilah from the Philistines and then discovers that the city may still betray him to Saul. He repeatedly seeks the LORD, who directs him away from capture and into the wilderness, where the Ziphites inform Saul of his location. The chapter shows David relying on God's guidance in every movement.
key_points: David rescues Keilah from the Philistines.
The LORD warns that Keilah may betray him.
David escapes before Saul arrives.
The Ziphites report his hiding place.
God keeps directing David's steps.

the LORD said, Go

the wilderness of Ziph

title: David spares Saul in the cave
summary_text: David has the chance to kill Saul in the cave at En-gedi but chooses instead to cut off a corner of Saul's robe and let God judge between them. Saul is briefly softened and acknowledges David's future kingship, yet the conflict is not truly resolved. The chapter shows David's restraint and trust in the LORD rather than revenge.
key_points: David refuses to kill Saul when he can.
He appeals to the LORD as judge.
Saul admits David's righteousness.
The two part in apparent peace.
David trusts God's timing for vindication.
evidence_phrases: the LORD's anointed
I will not put forth my hand
the LORD judge between me and you
you are more righteous than I
the LORD reward you good

title: Abigail and Nabal
summary_text: Samuel dies, and David moves deeper into the wilderness where Nabal insults him and refuses food for his men. Abigail quickly intervenes with provisions, speaks humbly to David, and prevents bloodshed by reminding him that the LORD will fight his battles. Nabal later dies after his drunken feast, and David receives Abigail as wife, turning restraint into mercy and provision into peace.
key_points: Samuel's death is marked before the Nabal story.
Nabal refuses David's request for provisions.
Abigail rushes out with gifts and a plea for mercy.
David turns from vengeance after hearing her words.
Nabal dies and Abigail becomes David's wife.
evidence_phrases: Samuel died
Who is David?
the LORD will certainly make my lord a sure house
the LORD has restrained my lord from bloodguiltiness
David took Abigail

title: David spares Saul again
summary_text: When Saul camps in the wilderness, David slips into the camp with Abishai and takes Saul's spear and water jug instead of killing him. From a distance he calls Saul to account, and Saul again admits that he has sinned and that David will surely prevail. The second chance underlines David's refusal to seize the throne by violence.
key_points: David enters Saul's camp by night.
He refuses Abishai's offer to kill Saul.
The spear and water jug become proof of restraint.
Saul confesses his wrongdoing again.
David leaves judgment to God.
evidence_phrases: a deep sleep from the LORD
the LORD's anointed
where is the king's spear
I have sinned
the LORD rewards every man

title: David lives among the Philistines
summary_text: David decides he will perish by Saul's hand unless he finds refuge among the Philistines, so he settles in Ziklag under Achish. From there he raids surrounding peoples while telling Achish that he has struck Judah, keeping the king of Gath confident in his loyalty. The chapter captures David living by concealment and survival in enemy territory.
key_points: David seeks refuge in Philistine territory.
Achish gives him Ziklag.
David raids neighboring peoples.
He misleads Achish about his targets.
The arrangement masks ongoing conflict.
evidence_phrases: I shall now perish one day by the hand of Saul
Achish gave him Ziklag

David went up, and invaded
he left neither man nor woman alive

title: Saul and the medium at Endor
summary_text: Saul becomes desperate when the Philistines gather and the LORD no longer answers him, so he disguises himself and seeks a medium at Endor. The woman summons Samuel, who rebukes Saul and repeats that the LORD has torn the kingdom away from him. Saul hears the sentence of death before returning to face the battle.
key_points: Saul seeks guidance after God is silent.
He disguises himself to visit the medium.
Samuel appears and speaks judgment.
Saul is told the kingdom is finished.
He falls into terror before battle.
evidence_phrases: I am sore distressed
bring me up Samuel

tomorrow you and your sons will be with me
the LORD has done to him

title: David sent back from the Philistine army
summary_text: As the Philistines assemble for war, their lords distrust David and insist that he not march with them. Achish defends David's loyalty, but the commanders fear he will turn against them in battle, so David is sent home from Aphek. The chapter frees David from an impossible battlefield alliance before the final crisis with Saul unfolds.
key_points: The Philistine leaders do not trust David.
Achish tries to defend him.
The lords refuse to let him fight with them.
David is sent back from the army.
His return prevents future conflict.

he has been good in my sight

the lords of the Philistines

title: Ziklag burned and recovered
summary_text: While David and his men are away, Amalekites raid Ziklag, burn the city, and take the families captive. David strengthens himself in the LORD, pursues the raiders with divine guidance, and recovers everyone along with much spoil. The chapter ends with David sharing the recovered goods widely, showing both courage and generosity.
key_points: Ziklag is burned and the families are taken.
David seeks strength and direction from the LORD.
The Egyptian servant guides them to the raiders.
David recovers all the captives and spoil.
The recovered goods are shared fairly.
evidence_phrases: David was greatly distressed
David strengthened himself in the LORD

nothing of theirs was missing
David came to the two hundred

title: Saul's death at Gilboa
summary_text: The Philistines defeat Israel at Mount Gilboa, and Saul's sons fall beside him. Wounded and unable to continue, Saul falls on his own sword after his armor bearer refuses to kill him, and the Philistines later display the bodies in shame. Men of Jabesh-gilead recover the remains and bury them with fasting, ending Saul's reign in grief and honor.
key_points: Israel is defeated on Mount Gilboa.
Saul's sons die beside him.
Saul kills himself after the armor bearer refuses.
The Philistines display the bodies in triumph.
Jabesh-gilead gives Saul and his sons a burial.
evidence_phrases: the men of Israel fled
fall on your sword

they fasted seven days
the Philistines stripped him
